cases.prompt: Marina del Rey Harbor entrance, CA
cases.center_description: entrance of Marina del Rey Harbor where it meets Santa Monica Bay
cases.label: Marina del Rey Harbor entrance at the jettied mouth opening into Santa Monica Bay, California
cases.reason: The request clearly identifies the specific coastal feature: the harbor/channel entrance of Marina del Rey where it opens to Santa Monica Bay between the entrance jetties. Using geographic knowledge of Marina del Rey’s main entrance on the Los Angeles County coast, a practical DEM center is the midpoint of that entrance opening, approximately 33.9768 N, 118.4519 W.

cases.center_description: harbor entrance where Santa Cruz Harbor opens to Monterey Bay
cases.label: Santa Cruz Harbor entrance to Monterey Bay, Santa Cruz, California
cases.reason: The only map candidate is an unrelated car repair shop and does not represent the requested feature, so it is rejected. The user's intent is the specific harbor mouth where Santa Cruz Harbor connects to Monterey Bay, not the harbor generally or a nearby business. With no valid candidate geometry available, resolving from general geographic knowledge is appropriate here at medium confidence; the proposed point is a reasonable center on the entrance between the jetties for a 2 km by 2 km DEM domain.

cases.center_description: entrance of Oceanside Harbor where the harbor meets the ocean
cases.label: Oceanside Harbor entrance between the jetties, Oceanside, California
cases.reason: The user's intent is the harbor entrance where Oceanside Harbor connects to the Pacific Ocean. The only map candidate is a generic point for the broader harbor/dock area and does not represent the requested inlet. Correcting away from that interior harbor point is appropriate. Based on the named place, the target is the ocean-facing entrance channel between the north and south jetties, and the proposed coordinate fits that specific feature better than the candidate centroid.

cases.center_description: harbor entrance channel between Morro Rock and the breakwater
cases.label: Morro Bay Harbor entrance channel between Morro Rock and the harbor breakwater, California
cases.reason: The geocoder candidates do not represent the requested feature: one is an unrelated street in Newport Beach and the others are civic buildings in Morro Bay, not the harbor entrance. The user’s intent is specific to the entrance channel gap between Morro Rock and the breakwater/jetty. With no usable map candidate for that feature, the proposed point is a reasonable text-resolved center for the harbor entrance area and is suitable for centering a 2 km by 2 km DEM domain.

cases.prompt: Mission Bay entrance, San Diego, CA
cases.center_description: Mission Bay entrance channel where Mission Bay opens to the Pacific Ocean
cases.label: Mission Bay entrance channel / inlet to the Pacific Ocean, San Diego, California
cases.reason: The geocoder candidate is for the broader bay and not the requested target, so its point should not be used directly. The user's intent is specifically the entrance channel where Mission Bay opens to the Pacific Ocean. Using general geographic knowledge, that inlet lies between the north and south jetties at the west end of Mission Bay near the Mission Beach/Ocean Beach area. The proposed point is a reasonable midpoint for centering a 2 km by 2 km DEM on the inlet/channel.

cases.center_description: entrance of Newport Harbor between the jetties at the ocean
cases.label: Newport Harbor entrance channel between the jetties, Newport Beach, California
cases.reason: The geocoder candidates are clearly unrelated to the requested feature and should be rejected. The user's intent is specific: the ocean entrance to Newport Harbor/Newport Bay at Newport Beach, specifically the navigational gap between the entrance jetties where the harbor meets the Pacific. With no valid map candidate for that feature, resolving from general geographic knowledge is appropriate here, and the proposed point appears to represent the harbor mouth rather than a generic harbor centroid or inland place.

cases.center_description: entrance of Brookings Harbor where the harbor opens to the Pacific Ocean
cases.label: Brookings Harbor entrance / jetty opening to the Pacific Ocean, Brookings, Oregon
cases.reason: The provided geocoder candidates are all clearly wrong-feature inland places (school, shopping center, clinic, school district office) and do not represent the requested harbor entrance. The user explicitly wants the entrance where Brookings Harbor opens to the Pacific Ocean, not a generic Brookings Harbor label or town feature. With no valid map candidate for the entrance itself, resolving from general geographic knowledge is appropriate here at medium confidence; the proposed point near the jetty opening at the Port of Brookings Harbor/Chetco mouth is consistent with the request.

cases.center_description: Yaquina Bay entrance between the jetties where it meets the Pacific Ocean
cases.label: Yaquina Bay entrance between the north and south jetties, Newport, Oregon
cases.reason: The returned candidate is a generic point for Yaquina Bay and not the requested simulation target. The user specifically wants the inlet mouth where Yaquina Bay meets the Pacific Ocean, between the north and south jetties, rather than the broader bay or town. With only a stale bay label available from the geocoder, the proposed corrected point at the navigation entrance is a reasonable practical center for a 2 km coastal DEM domain.

cases.center_description: entrance of Coos Bay at the Pacific Ocean between the jetties
cases.label: Coos Bay entrance between the north and south jetties
cases.reason: The available candidates do not represent the requested feature: one is the city/admin area and the other is a generic bay point, while the user explicitly wants the Pacific Ocean entrance at the mouth of Coos Bay between the north and south jetties near Charleston. The proposed point is a reasonable midpoint for that inlet and better matches the intended DEM target than either candidate. Evidence is indirect because no jetty/entrance geometry is provided, so confidence remains medium.

cases.center_description: mouth of New River Inlet where it enters the Atlantic Ocean
cases.label: New River Inlet mouth, Onslow County, North Carolina
cases.reason: The available map evidence contains a single candidate explicitly named New River Inlet in Onslow County, matching the requested feature. The user wants the ocean-facing mouth rather than a town or broad regional label, and this point appears to represent that inlet opening. There is no alternate geometry or endpoint evidence to justify moving it, so the proposed center is acceptable as the practical target for the 2 km DEM domain.

cases.center_description: shoreline at the USACE Field Research Facility research pier in Duck
cases.label: USACE Field Research Facility research pier shoreline, Duck, North Carolina
cases.reason: The available candidate is only the town of Duck administrative boundary, which does not represent the requested feature and should be rejected as the DEM center. The user explicitly wants the shoreline at the USACE Field Research Facility research pier. With insufficient direct map-feature evidence but adequate general geographic knowledge, the FRF pier is on the oceanfront in Duck and the proposed point places the center at the beach immediately adjacent to the pier rather than at the town centroid. This matches the practical coastal target better than the available candidate.

cases.prompt: Government Cut ocean entrance, Miami FL
cases.center_description: ocean entrance of Government Cut by South Beach where it meets the Atlantic
cases.label: Ocean entrance of Government Cut at the Atlantic side near South Beach, Miami Beach, Florida
cases.reason: The user wants the seaward/ocean entrance of Government Cut where the channel opens to the Atlantic, not a midpoint of the inlet. Candidate 0 is the full Government Cut canal as a LineString spanning west-to-east, with first coordinate at the inner harbor side (-80.1325074, 25.7633012) and last coordinate at the eastern Atlantic opening (-80.089902, 25.764571). That east endpoint matches the requested ocean entrance by South Beach. Candidate 1 is only a short western segment of the cut and does not reach the Atlantic mouth, so it should be rejected.

cases.center_description: Clearwater Pass inlet where it meets the Gulf of Mexico
cases.label: Clearwater Pass inlet mouth at the Gulf of Mexico, near Clearwater Beach/Sand Key, Florida
cases.reason: The user's target is the Gulf-facing mouth of Clearwater Pass, not the broader pass or an inland canal. Candidate 0 is the correct Clearwater Pass waterway spanning from the inlet interior to offshore; its LineString endpoint at (-82.8228992, 27.9617859) matches the seaward opening where the pass meets the Gulf of Mexico. Candidate 1 is a short segment of the same pass but represents only the inner/eastern portion terminating at the same mouth point. Candidate 2 is an unrelated canal farther north, and candidate 3 is only a generic point label for the strait. Using the shared seaward endpoint of the pass geometry best matches the requested inlet mouth location.

cases.center_description: Galveston harbor entrance between the jetties where the ship channel meets the Gulf
cases.label: Galveston Harbor entrance between the north and south jetties at the Gulf end of Galveston Ship Channel
cases.reason: The available map candidate is clearly unrelated—an artwork in downtown Houston—and does not represent the requested coastal feature. The user's intent is specific: the inlet between the entrance jetties where the Galveston Ship Channel meets the Gulf of Mexico. With no valid candidate geometry for the jetties, the best resolution is from general geographic knowledge, placing the center at the practical midpoint of the Galveston Entrance between the north and south jetties.

cases.prompt: Port Aransas ship channel entrance, TX
cases.center_description: Port Aransas ship channel entrance at Aransas Pass where it meets the Gulf of Mexico
cases.label: Port Aransas ship channel entrance at Aransas Pass, Gulf of Mexico side
cases.reason: The request identifies a specific, well-known coastal feature: the Port Aransas/Aransas Pass jettied ship-channel entrance where the Corpus Christi Ship Channel meets the Gulf of Mexico. A practical DEM center is the middle of the entrance between the north and south jetties on the Gulf side, near 27.833 N, 97.041 W.

cases.center_description: entrance of Pearl Harbor where the harbor meets the ocean
cases.label: Pearl Harbor seaward entrance/mouth
cases.reason: The user's target is the seaward entrance where Pearl Harbor connects to the Pacific, not the broader harbor polygon. Candidate 0 is explicitly 'Pearl Harbor Entrance Channel' with LineString geometry. Its line runs from inland near 21.3524693 to the ocean-facing end at the last coordinate (-157.9572147, 21.2999412). For a harbor mouth/channel request, the correct DEM center is the seaward endpoint of that geometry rather than the representative point or the general Pearl Harbor polygon in candidate 1.

cases.location: Ala Wai Boat Harbor, Honolulu, Hawaii
cases.center_description: entrance of Ala Wai Boat Harbor where it opens to the ocean
cases.label: Ala Wai Boat Harbor ocean entrance, Honolulu, Hawaii
cases.reason: Ala Wai Boat Harbor is a well-known small-boat harbor at Waikiki. The requested target is the seaward mouth on the south side where the breakwaters open to the Pacific, not the harbor centroid. A practical DEM center is the harbor entrance channel near approximately 21.2794 N, 157.8449 W.
